transcript: So, I went to my first surf competition last year, and honestly? It was nothing like I expected. I always thought these events would be really serious — you know, athletes with headphones on, nobody talking, that kind of tension you see in the Olympics. But it wasn't like that at all. There were families everywhere. Kids running around with boards way bigger than they were. Someone's grandmother selling homemade sandwiches out of a cooler. The competitors themselves were just sitting on the sand, chatting with each other between heats. What really got me was watching the waiting. Because that's most of what surfing is, actually — sitting out there, reading the water, waiting for the right wave. There was this one guy, he sat out there for almost fifteen minutes without catching anything. The crowd went completely quiet. And then he took off on this wave that didn't even look that special at first, and he made it look effortless. Everyone lost it. I remember thinking, that patience is the whole sport. Anyone can paddle. Knowing which wave is worth your energy — that's the hard part.
resumo_pt: Uma surfista conta como foi assistir sua primeira competição e o que a surpreendeu no ambiente.

glossario.significado_pt: nada parecido com o que eu esperava
glossario.exemplo: It was nothing like I expected.

glossario.significado_pt: baterias, as rodadas eliminatórias de uma competição
glossario.exemplo: chatting with each other between heats

glossario.significado_pt: o que mais me impressionou / me marcou
glossario.exemplo: What really got me was watching the waiting.

glossario.significado_pt: dropou, começou a surfar a onda
glossario.exemplo: he took off on this wave

glossario.significado_pt: todo mundo foi à loucura, vibrou muito
remar (com os braços, sobre a prancha)

glossario.exemplo: Knowing which wave is worth your energy

perguntas.pergunta: What was the speaker's main reaction to the competition?
opcoes: It was more competitive than she imagined
It was far more relaxed and social than she imagined
It was disappointing and poorly organised
It was too crowded to enjoy properly
perguntas.explicacao_pt: Ela esperava tensão de Olimpíadas, mas encontrou famílias, crianças correndo e competidores conversando na areia.

perguntas.pergunta: What does the speaker suggest is the most important skill in surfing?
opcoes: Staying calm in front of a crowd
perguntas.explicacao_pt: No final ela diz que qualquer um consegue remar — o difícil é saber qual onda vale o seu esforço.

perguntas.pergunta: How would you describe the speaker's tone?
perguntas.explicacao_pt: Ela usa 'honestly?', 'what really got me' e 'Everyone lost it' — linguagem calorosa de quem gostou e se surpreendeu.
O áudio diz: 'Someone's grandmother selling homemade sandwiches out of a cooler.'

perguntas.pergunta: How long did the surfer wait before catching a wave?
perguntas.explicacao_pt: 'he sat out there for almost fifteen minutes without catching anything.'

perguntas.pergunta: What did the crowd do while the surfer was waiting?
Why did the crowd react so strongly to that particular wave?
opcoes: It was the largest wave of the day
The surfer won the competition with it
The long wait made the payoff feel earned
The surfer had fallen on every previous attempt
perguntas.explicacao_pt: Ela nota que a onda não parecia especial no início — o que criou o momento foi a espera longa e o silêncio antes. Isso está implícito, não dito diretamente.
